title: So how do they dispose of Angels
display_title: So how do they dispose of Angels
wikitext:
[]: So how do they dispose of Angels?             Quote Post by Radical Lanzar » Thu Jan 19, 2012 4:18 pm No seriously. I think I remember a discussion about it on an elevator shaft between Misato and whomever. I also seem to remember various destruction scenes and construction crews shown in the general area (Eva accidents or dead angels) Thing is, don't these angels have some kind of odd DNA or body that can replicate/mutate itself? I'm surprised just shipping the individual parts off would be good enough.

[]:             Quote Post by JoeD80 » Thu Jan 19, 2012 4:35 pm There were some cranes and things taking apart angel #5 (Ramiel) in a few episodes. Maybe they just put them in the pit with the dead Evas. DNA doesn't replicate once it's dead.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Thu Jan 19, 2012 8:32 pm In Eva 2, Kaworu says that Angels don't decay after death, but the canocity of this game is debated. I don't remember anything else related to this ever being mentioned, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Stallard » Thu Jan 19, 2012 9:15 pm I remember seeing the giant crashed diamond angel several episodes after it was destroyed, so I assume they don't really make an effort to. Most angels blow up anyways.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jan 20, 2012 12:58 am TehDonutKing wrote: In Eva 2, Kaworu says that Angels don't decay after death

[]:             Quote Post by Someone » Fri Jan 20, 2012 2:22 am I see a huge non-rottable (might not be a word) food supply! And it might give you angel superpowers too! Or some kind of terrible disease.

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Fri Jan 20, 2012 4:03 am Wait, Ramiel wasn't made of steel?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jan 20, 2012 5:58 am LeoXiao wrote: Wait, Ramiel wasn't made of steel?

[]:             Quote Post by Radical Lanzar » Fri Jan 20, 2012 7:41 am Someone wrote: I see a huge non-rottable (might not be a word) food supply! And it might give you angel superpowers too! Or some kind of terrible disease.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Fri Jan 20, 2012 9:51 am Radical Lanzar wrote: I was going to make a crack about dissecting the angels for food, but there's something in the anime that says they and human's can't co-exist or something.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Fri Jan 20, 2012 1:10 pm I thought they didn't need to dispose them that much because they tend to explode when they die.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Fri Jan 20, 2012 1:17 pm Don't they use the remains for research, otherwise use them as fertilizer.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Fri Jan 20, 2012 5:22 pm Stryker wrote: I thought they didn't need to dispose them that much because they tend to explode when they die.

[]:             Quote Post by BulletShoot » Fri Jan 20, 2012 6:52 pm TehDonutKing wrote: Matarael didn't, and I don't think Ramiel or Sahaquiel did either.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Fri Jan 20, 2012 10:03 pm BulletShoot wrote: -Zeruel, was eaten.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Jan 20, 2012 10:42 pm Reichu wrote: (Just spend a little time thinking about how gross one measly deer carcass in the middle of summer can get, and then extrapolate that to a deer the size of several skyscrapers...)

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Fri Jan 20, 2012 11:52 pm The Angels that do explode may still leave trace parts behind like blood which would have to be decontaminated; judging by the use of hazmat gear when Nerv investigates the ground zero (places where the angels are destroyed) it can be assumed that the material composing the angels is considered bio hazardous. As in seen with Shamshel, Ramiel, and Bardiel (NGE and Rebuild) Nerv apparently "dismantles" the corpses and keeps valuable or intact samples. What is done with the rest is not stated but I would assume that once the corpse is broken up into small enough pieces it could be stored, similar to how nuclear waste is stored, storage locations could include secure UN facilities, dumped into the ocean, or some Nerv facility. Another possibility is that since the angel could be considered bio hazardous, the remains of the broken up corpse could be incinerated in a medical grade incinerator, similar to what hospitals and labs use to dispose of hazardous waste.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Sat Jan 21, 2012 12:33 am KnightmareX13 wrote: The Angels that do explode may still leave trace parts behind like blood which would have to be decontaminated; judging by the use of hazmat gear when Nerv investigates the ground zero (places where the angels are destroyed) it can be assumed that the material composing the angels is considered bio hazardous.

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Sat Jan 21, 2012 1:23 am I don't think angels are made up of carbon-based biological material like we are. I don't think biological decomposers could interact with them in a meaningful way.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Jan 21, 2012 7:36 am ...But, wasn't there that little line in ep 5 where someone says to keep Shamshel's core and throw away the rest because it had decayed too far to be useful?

I'm surprised just shipping the individual parts off would be good enough.
